status_notes: Initial research from Microsoft Entra documentation, 2026-04-04. Pass 1 (service docs). Needs Pass 2 (API), Pass 3 (cross-reference), and Pass 5 (human review with live Azure environment).
notes: Foundation identity service for Azure and Microsoft 365. Formerly Azure Active Directory (Azure AD), renamed to Microsoft Entra ID in 2023. Provides authentication, authorization, MFA, conditional access, identity protection, and privileged access management.

match_nodes.description: Pre-configured security baseline for Entra ID tenants

data.description: One-click security baseline for organizations. Requires MFA registration for all users, MFA for admins on every sign-in, MFA for all users when needed, blocks legacy authentication protocols, and protects privileged activities (Azure portal, PowerShell, CLI).

default.note: Enabled by default on tenants created after October 22, 2019. Disabled automatically if Conditional Access policies are configured.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use for organizations without complex requirements. Upgrade to Conditional Access for advanced scenarios that need granular policy control.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Overview → Properties → Manage security defaults

configure.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Overview → Properties → Manage security defaults → toggle on/off
configure.note: Requires Conditional Access Administrator or Security Administrator role

data.description: Second factor authentication supporting 15+ methods. Managed through Authentication methods policies. Methods range from phishing-resistant (passkeys, certificate-based) to legacy (SMS, voice call).

options.name: Passkeys (FIDO2)
options.description: Phishing-resistant passwordless auth using FIDO2 security keys or synced passkeys

options.name: Windows Hello for Business
options.description: Phishing-resistant biometric/PIN auth tied to Windows device

options.name: Platform Credential for macOS
options.description: Phishing-resistant auth using macOS Secure Enclave

options.name: Microsoft Authenticator (passwordless)
options.description: Phone sign-in with number matching, no password required

options.name: Microsoft Authenticator (push notification)
options.description: Push notification with number matching for MFA

options.name: Microsoft Authenticator (TOTP)
options.description: Software OATH token generating 6-digit codes

options.name: Authenticator Lite (in Outlook)
options.description: MFA capability embedded in Outlook mobile app

options.name: Hardware OATH tokens
options.description: Physical devices generating TOTP codes

options.name: Third-party software OATH tokens
options.description: Any TOTP-compatible authenticator app

options.name: Certificate-based authentication
options.description: Phishing-resistant auth using X.509 certificates on smartcards or devices

options.name: External authentication methods
options.description: Third-party MFA providers integrated via API

options.name: Temporary Access Pass (TAP)
options.description: Time-limited passcode for onboarding or recovery scenarios

options.name: SMS
options.description: One-time code sent via text message

options.name: Voice call
options.description: Automated phone call for verification

options.name: QR code sign-in
options.description: QR code-based authentication for frontline workers

options.name: Email one-time passcode
options.description: OTP sent to email, primarily for B2B guest users

data.vendor_recommendation: Use phishing-resistant methods: passkeys (FIDO2), Windows Hello for Business, certificate-based authentication. Microsoft recommends moving away from SMS and voice call methods.
data.recent_changes: SMS-based MFA still supported but Microsoft actively recommends migration to phishing-resistant alternatives. Number matching now enforced for Authenticator push notifications.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Security → Authentication methods

configure.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Security → Authentication methods → Policies

data.description: Policy engine that evaluates sign-in conditions (signals) and enforces access controls. Evaluates: user/group membership, cloud app, location (named/trusted), device platform, device state/compliance, client app type, sign-in risk level, user risk level. Enforces: block access, grant with conditions (require MFA, require compliant device, require app protection policy, require password change), session controls (sign-in frequency, persistent browser, app-enforced restrictions). Requires Entra ID P1 license.

options.description: Target specific users, groups, roles, or all users
Target specific applications or all cloud apps
Named locations, trusted IPs, country-based filtering
Windows, macOS, iOS, Android, Linux
Compliant, Hybrid Azure AD joined, or unmanaged
Browser, mobile app, desktop client, legacy auth clients
Real-time risk: low, medium, high (requires P2)
Cumulative risk: low, medium, high (requires P2)
Deny the sign-in entirely
Require multi-factor authentication
Device must be Intune-compliant
Must use an approved client application
Sign-in frequency, persistent browser, app-enforced restrictions

data.vendor_recommendation: Replace security defaults with Conditional Access for organizations with P1+ licenses. Use policy templates for common scenarios. Start in report-only mode before enforcing. Always exclude break-glass emergency access accounts.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Security → Conditional Access

configure.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Security → Conditional Access → New policy

match_nodes.description: Risk-based identity security with automated detection and remediation

data.description: Automated detection and remediation of identity-based risks. Detects risky sign-ins (unfamiliar locations, anonymous IP addresses, malware-linked IPs, atypical travel, token anomalies, password spray, credential stuffing) and risky users (leaked credentials, suspicious activity patterns, anomalous user behavior). Integrates with Conditional Access for automated remediation. Requires Entra ID P2 license.

options.detections: Anonymous IP, atypical travel, malware-linked IP, unfamiliar sign-in properties, password spray, credential stuffing, anomalous token, suspicious browser
Leaked credentials, anomalous user activity, possible attempt to access PRT, suspicious API traffic

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable user risk and sign-in risk policies to auto-remediate detected risks. Configure user risk policy to require password change at medium+ risk. Configure sign-in risk policy to require MFA at medium+ risk.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Security → Identity Protection

configure.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Security → Identity Protection → User risk policy / Sign-in risk policy
configure.note: Risk policies are configured as Conditional Access policies using sign-in risk and user risk conditions

data.description: Time-limited, approval-based activation of privileged roles. Provides just-in-time access to Entra ID and Azure resources. Features: eligible vs active role assignments, time-bound activation (configurable duration), approval workflows, MFA enforcement on activation, justification requirements, notification on activation, and full audit trail. Requires Entra ID P2 license.

options.description: User is eligible but must activate the role before using it
User has the role permanently (time-bound or permanent)
Designated approvers must approve activation
MFA required on role activation
User must provide reason for activation
User must provide ticket number for activation

data.vendor_recommendation: Use PIM for all privileged roles. Require approval and MFA for activation of high-impact roles (Global Administrator, Privileged Role Administrator). Set maximum activation duration. Review eligible assignments periodically.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Identity Governance → Privileged Identity Management

configure.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Identity Governance → Privileged Identity Management → Entra ID roles → Settings

data.description: Scheduled reviews of user access to groups, applications, and roles. Reviewers (managers, resource owners, self-reviewers, or designated reviewers) confirm or deny continued access. Supports auto-apply of results and removal of access for non-responded reviews. Requires Entra ID P2 license.

options.description: Review who belongs to security or Microsoft 365 groups
Review who has access to enterprise applications
Review privileged role assignments (integrates with PIM)
Review Azure RBAC role assignments
Review entitlement management access package assignments

data.vendor_recommendation: Create recurring access reviews for privileged roles and sensitive group memberships. Enable auto-apply to automatically remove access when reviewers deny or don't respond.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Identity Governance → Access reviews

configure.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Identity Governance → Access reviews → New access review

match_nodes.description: Password policy and banned password enforcement

data.description: Custom banned password lists and smart lockout. Blocks common/weak passwords and their variations using a global banned password list (maintained by Microsoft from real telemetry) plus a custom banned password list (up to 1000 entries). Smart lockout tracks failed sign-in attempts and locks accounts after a threshold. Extends to on-premises Active Directory via password protection agents.

options.description: Up to 1000 custom terms that are blocked along with variations
Number of failed sign-ins before lockout
Duration of first lockout, increases with subsequent lockouts
Deploy password protection agents to Active Directory domain controllers

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable the custom banned password list with organization-specific terms (company name, products, locations). Deploy password protection to on-premises AD if using hybrid identity.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Security → Authentication methods → Password protection

configure.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Security → Authentication methods → Password protection → Edit
configure.note: On-premises deployment requires installing Azure AD Password Protection Proxy and DC Agent

match_nodes.description: Built-in and custom Entra ID roles for administrative access control

data.description: Role-based access control for Entra ID administration and Azure resource management. 70+ built-in roles ranging from Global Administrator (full access) to narrow roles like Authentication Administrator or Groups Administrator. Custom roles available with P1+ license for fine-grained permission combinations.

options.description: Full access to all Entra ID features. Limit to fewer than 5 users.
Read security info and reports, manage Entra ID and Office 365 security configuration
Manage Conditional Access policies
Manage users and groups, including password resets
Manage role assignments and PIM settings
Manage app registrations and enterprise apps
Manage authentication methods for non-admin users
User-defined roles with specific permission selections (P1+ license)

data.vendor_recommendation: Use least-privilege roles. Limit Global Administrator assignments to fewer than 5. Use PIM for just-in-time activation of privileged roles. Prefer narrow built-in roles over Global Administrator.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Roles and administrators

configure.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Roles and administrators → {role} → Add assignments

data.description: Non-human identities for applications, automation, and services. Includes app registrations (multi-tenant or single-tenant application objects), managed identities (Azure-managed, no secrets to handle), and federated credentials (workload identity federation with external identity providers like GitHub Actions, Kubernetes, AWS, GCP).

options.description: Auto-managed identity tied to an Azure resource lifecycle. Created and deleted with the resource. No secrets to manage.
Standalone managed identity that can be assigned to multiple Azure resources. Independent lifecycle.
Application with a secret string for authentication. Secrets have expiration dates.
Application authenticating with X.509 certificate. More secure than client secrets.
No secrets. External identity provider (GitHub, Kubernetes, AWS, GCP) authenticates directly. Uses OIDC token exchange.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use managed identities where possible (no secrets to manage). Use workload identity federation for external workloads (GitHub Actions, other clouds). Avoid client secrets — prefer certificates or federated credentials. Set short expiration on any secrets that must be used.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → App registrations / Enterprise applications
audit.cli: az ad app list / az ad sp list

configure.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → App registrations → New registration
configure.cli_federation: az ad app federated-credential create --id {app-id} --parameters credential.json

match_nodes.description: Application consent governance and admin approval workflow

data.description: Controls how users can grant permissions to applications. By default, users can consent to apps requesting low-risk permissions. Admin consent workflow allows users to request access to apps requiring admin-level permissions, routing requests to designated approvers. Prevents unauthorized permission grants that could lead to data exfiltration.

values: Allow user consent for apps from verified publishers, for selected permissions
Allow user consent for all apps
options.default: Allow user consent for apps from verified publishers, for selected permissions

options.description: Enable workflow for users to request admin consent
Users or groups who review admin consent requests

data.vendor_recommendation: Restrict user consent to verified publishers and low-risk permissions. Enable admin consent workflow so users can request access rather than being blocked. Review consent requests promptly.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Enterprise applications → Consent and permissions

configure.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Enterprise applications → Consent and permissions → User consent settings / Admin consent requests
